text:
It was a Saturday afternoon, and Sam was hanging out at the park with his dog. They were enjoying a picnic when they noticed a group of kids trying to catch fireflies. The dog, curious as ever, sniffed around and found a particularly bright one.

Sam reached out and plucked the fly from the grass, giggling. "Look, Max, a firefly!" But after a second it flew away. "Oh well," Sam said. "Maybe you should catch one yourself!"

The little kid looked disappointed. "They're too fast," he complained. "Mommy says there are no more."

Sam shrugged. "Sometimes dreams just slip away."

With a wagging tail, the dog trotted off, finding its own fireflies. Watching the stars peek out from between leaves, Sam thought about how fleeting wishes often are. He picked up his napkin.

"You know," he said aloud, biting into a berry, "I wish I could do more for those kids.
